version_changes.diff_summary: Added performance-contingent insomnia as an explicit sub-pattern (multiple authors: Spare_Butterfly_1256 'Insomnia when I have plans,' ThrowRA_cheesestix 'pressure to fall asleep before work') with matching patterns ('can't sleep when I have plans,' 'pressure to sleep before work'). Added the 'clean sleep study' sub-frame (ArguREBECCA, feelgoodsad, First_Rip_2152 all reference studies that found nothing) as a receptivity marker and added 'sleep study found nothing' / 'they said everything was fine' patterns. Added patterns from directly observed phrasing: 'the second I get into bed,' 'tired all day wired at night,' 'stuck in this loop,' 'sleep restriction felt like punishment,' 'therapist keeps adding more techniques,' 'felt completely rational at the time,' 'never slept alone,' 'sleep paralysis episodes,' 'am I focusing on my breathing too much.' Acknowledged the desperation-substance tail (THC/CBN, clonidine, hydroxyzine, 'za') as still in-segment when framed emotionally, while adding Dayvigo/tart-cherry dosing-question posts to negatives since those authors read as pure pharmacology-shoppers rather than the anxiety/rumination core.

Added first-person-POV mechanics, script-to-prose/zero-draft transliteration, and pre-project dread ('queasiness of embarking') as observed procedural pains; added specific unoriginality evidence (older foreign show with near-identical twist and title) and the 'Hozier self-titled album feel' as a named derivative-vibe pattern. Introduced a new 'craft-humility reset' cluster ('knew nothing about art', 'fundamentals felt boring', 'doomed writer' who rarely reads) and a 'storyteller crossing mediums' identity note (comics/animation/games). Added hubs signal for local open mics as a songwriter testing ground, and patterns like 'writing in the Notes app', 'how does first person even work', and 'looking for a cowriter or mentor' backed by specific posts.

Added nervous-system/safety quotes observed verbatim in samples ('mind feels severely unsafe', 'body didn't get the message that everything is okay') and the sleep-onset heart-racing / pulse-jump pattern (50→88 bpm), since multiple authors self-track this with smartwatches in lay terms. Added sleep-phobia and nightmare/hypnagogic-avoidance signals ('fear I won't wake up', 'afraid of nightmares', 'hypnagogic hallucinations keeping me up') after 4+ posts on that sub-frame. Added failed-CBT-I / failed-sleep-restriction and resentment-toward-normal-sleepers signals, and the 'monks sleep good' aspirational frame from the technical-protocol requester. Added biohacker-adjacent terms (CJC-1295, Ipamorelin, DSIP, Whoop, HRV baseline) to negatives to keep this segment cleanly separated from Tier 1.

Added first-time-novelist procedural-scaffolding cluster (word-count goals, genre identification, POV mechanics, interlude pacing) after 5+ posts explicitly framed as 'my very first novel' or 'I started writing 2 weeks ago.' Added 'dream-adjacent subject matter' observation (literal American Dreams painting, nightmare-as-artwork, magical-squirrel cult kid, 'dreams all coming true' lyrics) as direct Dust-fit signal. Added 'come out cringey when chasing [artist] vibe' and 'can only come up with 4 lines' as songwriter-specific patterns, and 'experimental storytelling' / 'interlude chapters' / 'figuring out my genre' as writer patterns. Added unmedicated-ADHD and ace-identity notes to identity signal. Negative-patterned 'greatest songwriters of all time list' to filter out the list-dump poster archetype.

Added grow-kit/cultivation vocabulary (wet yield, flushes, B+, Cambodian, lemon tek) after seeing 4+ authors post detailed home-grow logs. Added 'not Limitless' / 'Bradley Cooper in Limitless' and 'Ascension of Unc' as active negative/anti-framings users explicitly mock (Bryan Johnson critique post, fsavino correction post). Added colloquial anxiety metaphors 'brain noise' and 'eating up my RAM' (Ecstatic_Cell_981) and CPTSD/migraine as self-treatment vectors (JimmyEatReality, Prestigious_Fall_174). Added Penrose/Faggin and 'collective consciousness' to analytical register, 'my guru' to contemplative-crossover slice, and 'spectral journey'/'music-guided' as emerging trip-report framing.

Added newly observed self-coined technique acronyms (BILD, CAN-WILD, DEILD) and pattern strings ('strength sapped away', 'spawning people', 'Fujitora', 'Sun Song', 'persistent dream world', 'Day ??? of learning lucid dreaming', 'prompt for your brain', 'deconcentration') after multiple authors used them verbatim. Expanded sub-type 4 to explicitly call out self-branded guide authorship and added Soviet/esoteric-text framing as an emergent sub-pattern. Added 'fading in and out when hyper-aware' and 'alarm wakes too aggressively' to pain points, and added podcasts and esoteric texts to spend patterns based on direct samples.

Added explicit '2-5am' early-morning-awakening frame and cortisol/heart-rate-surge language after multiple posts (Aleksandr_Ulyev, No_Investigator_7889) described it precisely. Added 'sleep became a project/war/goal' and 'falling asleep is about letting go' framing from Thick_Set794, plus the self-monitoring meta-problem ('watching myself fall asleep') from thehustlerofi, and the sleep-phobia / fear-of-transition-into-sleep sub-frame from No-Math3120 and NicoleASUstudent. Elevated requests for a 'technical,' 'precise-steps,' clock-like mental protocol to a first-class pattern since two high-scoring authors asked for it verbatim. Added negative patterns for reversed sleep schedule, caffeine-was-the-cause, reflux/laryngospasm, and hunger-wakings — all present in sample but off-segment.

Added an 'unoriginality anxiety' cluster (songwriters accidentally recreating existing melodies; novelists fearing being scooped) and a 'somatic/emotional engagement' cluster (chest tightness, resistance to opening the doc) — both appeared in 4+ sampled posts and map directly to Dust's value prop. Added patterns 'hazy narration', 'events aren't told chronologically', 'gardener or pantser', 'convoluted first book', 'accidentally remaking another song', 'resistance to opening the doc', 'spurts of poop'. Expanded identity to include chronic-illness/caregiving constraints (Hashimoto's, mom of young kid) as drivers of procrastination. Added negative pattern for promotional collaboration DMs and paid-course pitches observed in noise.

Added multi-word phrases actually observed across samples: 'we are all one,' 'cosmic joke,' 'shadow figures,' 'bottom-up vs top-down,' 'cyclic sighing'/'Balban,' 'Mandukya Upanishad,' 'McKennaii,' 'amanita microdosing,' 'high-masking autistic,' 'executive dysfunction,' 'ketamine infusions,' 'repressed emotions bubbling up,' 'vivid dreams,' 'microdose vs SSRI,' and 'Lumenate.' Added negative patterns for biohacker-longevity framing ('longevity therapy,' 'another intervention in the stack') after the Bryan Johnson critique post, plus 'just accept the anxiety.' Expanded definition to surface the microdose-vs-SSRI decision point, the ketamine-infusion crossover, and the vivid-dreams/emotional-surfacing signal (direct Dust wedge).

Added STM/'no village' vocabulary and patterns (DilemmaKingdom, Adventurous_Cow_2129, dumbbitch4sure) alongside existing FTM framing; expanded patterns to cover specific recurring themes seen across samples — breech, emergency c-section, Zofran, raspberry leaf tea, water broke, mucus plug, preeclampsia, diastasis recti, postpartum hair loss, MIL/SIL, baby name, baby shower, exclusively pumping, wake window. Added pain-point categories for postpartum body grief, intimacy/partner strain, in-law boundary stress, and lost friendships, all of which appeared in multiple samples but weren't represented in v2.

Added patch-specific titration language ('.0375', '.05 patch', 'bumped up', 'dialing in'), brand names actually used by these authors (Estradot, Utrogestan, Defy), and several newly-prominent pain points from the sample: drenching night sweats that soak bedding, escalating estrogen tolerance ('dose keeps needing to go up every 2 months'), patch supply/formulation disruption (Sandoz Estradot rebrand), spotting/thickened-lining scares, frozen shoulder and joint pain, and the 'two useless weeks a month' work-productivity framing. Added Dayvigo/Lunesta as the insurance-gated sleep meds that push women into CBT-I. Added 'estrogen sensitive' and 'blank mid-sentence' as specific phrases. Kept all prior structural facts and core patterns unchanged.

Added stabilization-adjacent pain points observed in this batch ('can't move fast / underwater feeling', chained nightmares with in-dream paralysis on RC, recall regression, slow sleep-onset blocking techniques) and promoted hypnagogic-transition fear with concrete symptoms (300 BPM, entity hallucinations, 'pulled into the abyss'). Added patterns 'dream portal', 'portal fusion', 'pinch my nose', 'chained nightmares', 'bucket list', 'asked Claude/ChatGPT', 'brain is like an AI' based on multiple author references; strengthened AI-as-coach signal from 'worth tracking' to explicit recurring behavior. Added 'alien abduction' / 'astral abduction' to negatives after seeing a non-fit crossover post in r/DreamInterpretation.

Elevated the builder/tinkerer trait from 'meaningful share' to defining behavior after seeing 8+ authors shipping open-source aggregators (WearSync, Calibrate, Keld, Garmin Health companion app) and naming Terra API pricing as the forcing function. Added statistical-methods vocabulary (Granger causality, association rule mining, relative amplitude, interdaily stability) and npj Digital Medicine as a citation hub, per HankWilliams42, thesnoopdawg, and NoParsleyForYou. Expanded Eight Sleep pain detail with specific failure modes (cover leaks, pump failures, hub incompatibility, cloud-dependency with no local fallback) and concrete $500-$1,100 replacement costs from three separate posts. Added peptide expansion (CJC-1295, Ipamorelin, BPC-157, TB-500) and sleep-audio hardware (Somnipods, Ozlo, A30) as emerging sub-categories. Added GitHub to community hubs.

author_overlap.note: Authors almost never span multiple segments at composite >= 2.5. The adjacency signal is carried by shared language across audiences, not shared people.

partnership_ranking.note: Ranked by qualified-author density, not post volume. Subreddits with fewer than 10 unique authors excluded.

clinical.demographic_inference: Adults across a wide age range (late 20s through retirement), disproportionately including veterans, first responders, bereaved partners, and assault survivors; geography varies but skews toward regions with active VA, hospice, and trauma-therapy networks.
clinical.profession_hints: Signal is sparse but clusters around military and first-responder service histories, caregiving roles, and people on medical leave or disability; income and employment status are often disrupted by the condition itself.
competing_products: prazosin and other prescribed nightmare medications
cannabis and alcohol for sleep suppression
clinical.what_they_want_from_dream_tech: A way to sleep through the night without reliving the worst moment of their life, ideally without another pill or another clinician to explain it to.
representative_quotes: I am afraid to fall asleep because I know what is waiting for me.
The medication dulls the dreams but I wake up feeling like I have not slept at all.
It has been months since they died and I still see them every night — I do not know if I want that to stop or not.
clinical.activation_hook: Earned-media and clinician-partnership storytelling (trauma therapists, VA-adjacent providers, hospice and grief organizations) that positions DUST as a tool introduced through a trusted caregiver, never through direct outreach to the sufferer.

biohackers.demographic_inference: Predominantly male, ages 25-50 with a long tail into 50s, urban and tech-hub concentrated across US metros, mid-to-late career or endurance-athlete life stage.
biohackers.profession_hints: Tech-adjacent knowledge workers and builders — engineers, product people, and technically literate hobbyists with $75K-$300K+ income who self-serve with APIs and spreadsheets.
competing_products: DIY dashboards (Athlytic, Bevel, custom API aggregators, home EEG rigs)
biohackers.what_they_want_from_dream_tech: An open, data-layer product that augments their existing stack with genuinely novel, time-lagged insight — something the wearable incumbents structurally can't deliver.
representative_quotes: Fourteen months of religious tracking, 65-degree room, magnesium glycinate, spreadsheet exports every Sunday — and my score still hovered in the 70s until I put the ring in a drawer.
I wear a Garmin, my training partners wear Whoop and Apple Watch, and nothing talks to anything, so I ended up building the aggregator myself.
The AI coach in the app can't even read its own data, and the subscription keeps going up — I'm done paying to have my own metrics held hostage.
biohackers.activation_hook: Position DUST as the first dream-layer API that plugs into the stack they already built — surfacing REM-phase and subjective dream signal their Oura, WHOOP, and Eight Sleep feeds structurally miss.

lucid.demographic_inference: Male-skewing, ages 25-40, college-educated, likely urban or college-town North America, past the beginner phase of a long-running hobby.
lucid.profession_hints: Knowledge-worker or student adjacent — fluent in technical jargon, comfortable consulting AI chatbots as coaches, and treating practice like a skill tree to optimize.
competing_products: dream journals and WBTB/SSILD/FILD protocol stacks
lucid.what_they_want_from_dream_tech: A reliable way to stabilize and extend lucidity past the first few minutes and gain fine-grained in-dream control, not another induction trick.
representative_quotes: I can get lucid fine, but the moment I realize it the dream collapses or I snap awake from excitement.
Ten years in and I'm hitting a ceiling — induction isn't the problem, it's duration and being able to actually move through the dream.
I've been stacking techniques and even asking an AI to coach me through stabilization, but nothing holds for more than five minutes.
lucid.activation_hook: Frame DUST as the stabilization and duration tool for practitioners who already induce reliably — the final-boss layer above Galantamine and Shape, not a beginner entry point.

perimenopause.demographic_inference: Woman aged 41-48, mid-career, US-based, likely suburban, often juggling young-to-teen kids and aging parents (sandwich-generation load).
perimenopause.profession_hints: College-educated knowledge worker — writers, professionals, caregivers — in peak earning years where cognitive output directly drives income, making brain fog a career-threatening symptom.
competing_products: Estrogel and testosterone gel via telehealth menopause clinics (Midi, Maven, Gennev, Elektra)
Vaginal estradiol cream and hormonal IUDs
Self-managed routines: dry erase boards, rigid schedules, supplement stacks
perimenopause.what_they_want_from_dream_tech: A non-pharmaceutical sleep and dream adjunct that fills the gaps her HRT can't close — especially 3am wakings, REM fragmentation, and progesterone-grogginess tradeoffs — without asking her to abandon the hormone protocol she fought to get.
representative_quotes: HRT mostly works but I'm still waking at 4am with my heart pounding, and no provider seems to take that piece seriously.
I used to be the sharp one at work — now I lose words mid-sentence and it's terrifying because my job depends on writing.
Progesterone saved my sleep but I'm groggy until noon; I'm tired of choosing between sedation and insomnia.
perimenopause.activation_hook: Position as the missing layer for women whose HRT got them 70% of the way there — meeting them mid-titration as a tool for the sleep architecture and cognitive recovery that hormones alone don't fully restore.

pregnancy.demographic_inference: Woman aged 25-35, middle-class, likely college-educated, in second or third trimester of her first pregnancy across suburban US.
pregnancy.profession_hints: Employment signals are thin, but the register (clinical shorthand, insurance/OB navigation, access to specialist care) suggests white-collar or healthcare-adjacent work on maternity runway.
competing_products: meditation/sleep apps like Expectful and Calm
pregnancy.what_they_want_from_dream_tech: Something that quiets the 2am mental spiral about the baby without adding another thing to feel guilty about taking while pregnant.
representative_quotes: I'm 33 weeks and every time I close my eyes my brain runs worst-case scenarios about labor — I can't shut it off.
I love this baby but I hate what pregnancy is doing to my body and I feel awful even saying that out loud.
It's 2am, I'm wedged between pillows, baby's kicking my ribs, and I'm doomscrolling induction stories again.
pregnancy.activation_hook: Position it as a safe, non-pharmaceutical way to stop the 3am worry loop — framed around 'can't sleep, mind racing, weird pregnancy dreams' rather than dream journaling — and seed it through Peanut and trusted FTM micro-influencers who already hold this audience's trust.

consciousness.demographic_inference: Late 20s to mid-40s, college-educated, coast-skewed US, likely in a knowledge-work life stage with enough stability to treat inner exploration as a serious project.
consciousness.profession_hints: Signals suggest white-collar or creative-analytical work — comfortable citing research, writing long-form, and budgeting for supplements, retreats, and guided sessions.
competing_products: psilocybin macrodoses and microdosing protocols (Stamets stack, Golden Teacher, Penis Envy)
Vipassana, metta, and Yoga Nidra practice
breathwork and contemplative apps (Lumenate, cyclic sighing protocols)
consciousness.what_they_want_from_dream_tech: A reliable, drug-free on-ramp to the same ego-loosening, insight-generating states they currently chase through plant medicine — without the logistics, legality, or comedown.
representative_quotes: My LSD sessions give me top-down pattern recognition; psilocybin drops me into bottom-up somatic material — I want tools that know the difference.
The heroic dose wasn't escapism, it was an inward journey, and the real work is the integration that happens in the weeks after.
Metta radiates outward, sankalpa plants inward, and ego death sits somewhere between — I'm trying to understand these states as one map.
consciousness.activation_hook: Frame DUST as an integration and non-dual-state practice — the 'natural psychedelic' that extends trip work into the dream layer — and let them self-experiment with protocols rather than handing them a start-low-go-slow script.

creatives.demographic_inference: Late 20s to late 30s, likely US or UK-based, in a life stage with enough stability to pursue long-form creative projects but not yet professionally established.
creatives.profession_hints: Day jobs outside the arts subsidize unpublished novels, unreleased songs, or personal visual work; creative output is avocational-but-serious rather than income-generating.
competing_products: Scrivener and Google Docs for drafting
voice memos for melody and lyric capture
creatives.what_they_want_from_dream_tech: A low-stakes way to generate raw, weird, emotionally charged material they can later shape, without the pressure of facing a blank page consciously.
representative_quotes: I've known this plot turn was coming for months but now that I'm actually writing it I'm an emotional wreck — is that normal?
Finished the draft and the high lasted about sixty seconds before I remembered redrafts, pitching, and all the rejection ahead.
Haven't written anything I actually liked in months; how do you get unstuck when the block becomes the identity?
creatives.activation_hook: Frame Dust as a sketchbook for your subconscious — a way to mine zero-draft material and surprise yourself out of a block, not a wellness app.

sleep_general.demographic_inference: Likely mid-20s to mid-40s adults, mixed gender, probably skewing US/UK/Western English-speaking based on subreddit norms.
sleep_general.profession_hints: Sparse occupational signal; posts reference impact on work performance and daytime functioning rather than specific careers, suggesting knowledge/office workers whose cognitive output is being eroded.
competing_products: sleep hygiene rituals (no screens, cut caffeine)
counting games, meditation apps, and self-invented mental tricks
sleep_general.what_they_want_from_dream_tech: A repeatable, in-the-moment mental practice that occupies the racing mind and signals safety to the body — something to do at 2am instead of lying there spiraling.
representative_quotes: I've tried every checklist item out there and I'm starting to wonder if my body just doesn't feel safe enough to sleep.
My body is wrecked but my brain is doing cardio — tired all day, wired the second my head hits the pillow.
Long-term bad sleep changes your personality in ways nobody warns you about; at 3am you genuinely believe your life is broken.
sleep_general.activation_hook: Position DUST not as another sleep aid but as a guided mental protocol that gives their racing mind somewhere to go — a structured 'what to do when you can't sleep' practice, not another hygiene rule.
